summary: Added OwloryUITests/HomeProtocolRunStepRegression as Lane 2 Batch 5, separate from HomeProtocolRegression (Batch 4 archive/restore). The one test (testSeededProtocolRunStepCompleteTransitionsOutOfPending) reuses the existing --owlory-ui-seed-home-protocol-run-continue-item seed, opens the active run sheet through the Today Continue row, taps the per-step Complete action, and asserts the Complete button disappears (proving the step transitioned out of pending) while the step title remains visible. Added accessibility identifiers home.protocolRun.step.<uuid> with .accessibilityElement(children: .contain) on the step row and home.protocolRun.step.action.complete.<uuid> on the Complete button in HomeView's ProtocolRunSheet. Did not add identifiers for skip, revert, archive, schedule, or template-edit controls. Extended make ui-regression: bare invocation runs all five regression classes; DOMAIN=home now runs both Home regression classes. make ui-regression DOMAIN=home passed in 31.8s (2 tests).

contract_status_changes.contract: Home protocol run step Complete action UI proof
contract_status_changes.before: TodayContinueRegression covered the Today Continue -> active run sheet route. Once inside the sheet, step-level interactions (Complete, Skip, Revert) had no Lane 2 UI proof. The home.protocolRun.sheet.<uuid> identifier existed but no step-level identifiers. HomeProtocolRegression (Batch 4) covered protocol template archive/restore management, not per-step actions inside a run.
contract_status_changes.after: HomeProtocolRunStepRegression covers the per-step Complete action on the first pending step of a seeded active run. New accessibility identifiers home.protocolRun.step.<uuid> and home.protocolRun.step.action.complete.<uuid> are reachable by XCUITest. make ui-regression DOMAIN=home runs both HomeProtocolRegression and HomeProtocolRunStepRegression; bare make ui-regression runs all five classes.

residual_risks: The assertion is that the Complete button disappears after tap. This is a proxy for 'step transitioned out of pending' because the Button is rendered only when step.status == .pending. A future refactor that renders Complete in other states would let the test falsely pass.
Only the Complete action has an accessibility identifier. Skip and revert are not covered by this batch; their identifiers were not added per slice scope.
Only one step is exercised. Multi-step sequential progression is documented as out-of-scope; adding it would require a new multi-step seed arg under OwloryUITestSupport.
The seeded single-step protocol cannot prove 'second step remains the only pending step' from the slice notes; that assertion was vacuous and skipped.

Accessibility identifiers and labels coexist on the same view: home.protocolRun.step.<uuid> (identifier, this slice) plus home.protocol.step.accessibility.complete.<uuid> (label, prior slice). Reviewers should not confuse the namespaces.
The test depends on the Today Continue -> active run sheet route smoke-covered by TodayContinueRegression. Failures in that route would surface as a different error shape here.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop is valid. Remaining Home-protocols sub-behaviors (step skip, step revert, schedule-window status, protocol template editing) are documented as deferred. Patterns and localization layout remain deferred per the Batch 5 triage. Each would need its own scoped triage before queueing.
